skill.name: writing-plans
skill.description: Use when you have a spec or requirements for a multi-step task, before touching code
skill.summary: Create detailed implementation plans with bite-sized tasks for developers new to your codebase
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static analysis detected 20 potential issues (16 external command patterns, 4 weak cryptography flags). All are false positives from markdown code spans and code blocks in documentation. This is a documentation-only skill with no executable code, network operations, or security risks. Safe to publish.

content.user_title: Generate detailed implementation plans for multi-step coding tasks
content.value_statement: Transform specifications into actionable implementation plans with granular tasks. This skill bridges the gap between requirements and code by creating comprehensive documentation that assumes zero prior context.
actual_capabilities: Create comprehensive implementation plans from requirements or specifications
Break down complex features into granular 2-5 minute tasks
Generate complete code examples for tests and implementations
Document exact file paths and line numbers for all modifications
Provide specific test commands with expected output for verification
Structure plans following TDD, DRY, and YAGNI principles
limitations: Requires existing specification or requirements to work from
Cannot execute code or verify implementations directly
Plans are static documents that may need updates if requirements change
Relies on accurate information about codebase structure and tooling

use_cases.title: Onboarding new developers
use_cases.description: Create detailed plans that help developers unfamiliar with your codebase implement features correctly without requiring constant supervision

use_cases.title: Complex feature implementation
use_cases.description: Break down multi-step features into manageable tasks with clear testing and verification steps for systematic development

use_cases.title: Test-driven development workflows
use_cases.description: Generate TDD task sequences that guide developers through writing tests first, implementing code, and verifying functionality

prompt_templates.title: Basic implementation plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an implementation plan for adding user authentication to our web application. Use the writing-plans skill to break this down into bite-sized tasks.
prompt_templates.scenario: Starting a new feature from requirements

prompt_templates.title: Refactoring plan
prompt_templates.prompt: Use writing-plans to create a detailed plan for refactoring the payment processing module. Include tests, file changes, and migration steps.
prompt_templates.scenario: Planning code refactoring with safety nets

prompt_templates.title: API integration plan
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to integrate the Stripe API for subscription management. Use writing-plans to create a complete implementation plan with all necessary steps.

prompt_templates.title: Multi-component feature
prompt_templates.prompt: Create an implementation plan for our real-time notification system that includes database changes, API endpoints, background workers, and frontend components.
prompt_templates.scenario: Complex features spanning multiple system layers

output_examples.input: Create a plan for adding password reset functionality
output: **Goal:** Allow users to reset passwords via email link
**Architecture:** Token-based reset flow with expiration, using existing email service
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN password_reset_token TEXT;
ALTER TABLE users ADD COLUMN password_reset_expires TIMESTAMP;

output_examples.input: Plan the implementation of a REST API endpoint
output: **Step 2: Run test to verify it fails**

best_practices: Always start with the required plan header including goal, architecture, and tech stack to provide complete context
Break tasks into the smallest possible units (2-5 minutes each) so developers can make steady progress with frequent commits
Include exact file paths and complete code examples in plans to eliminate ambiguity and decision-making during implementation
Specify expected test output at each step so developers can verify their work before proceeding
anti_patterns: Writing vague instructions like 'add validation' or 'update the handler' without specifying exact code and file locations
Creating large tasks that combine multiple steps, which makes progress tracking and error isolation difficult
Skipping test cases or expected outputs, which forces developers to guess what success looks like
Omitting the plan header or context sections, assuming the implementer has background knowledge

faq.question: When should I use this skill?
faq.answer: Use this skill when you have a specification or requirements document for a multi-step feature and need to create a detailed implementation plan before writing any code.

faq.question: What makes a task bite-sized?
faq.answer: A bite-sized task takes 2-5 minutes to complete and represents a single action like writing one test, running it, implementing minimal code, or making a commit.

faq.question: Do I need to follow TDD with this skill?
faq.answer: The skill encourages TDD practices (write test, verify failure, implement, verify success, commit) but you can adapt the task structure to your workflow.

faq.question: What happens after the plan is created?
faq.answer: After saving the plan, you can choose subagent-driven execution in the same session or a parallel session using the executing-plans skill for batch execution.

faq.question: Where are plans saved?
faq.answer: Plans are saved to `docs/plans/YYYY-MM-DD-<feature-name>.md` with a standardized filename format for easy reference.

faq.question: Can I modify plans after creation?
faq.answer: Yes, plans are living markdown documents. Update them if requirements change or if implementation reveals missing steps.
